steps.text: Determine whether fuel was inspected, handled and maintained as required, since the last inspection.
Determine whether the licensee’s fuel handling procedures are adequate to perform intended functions.
Determine whether fuel is moved and inspected consistent with the requirements of the TS and the licensee’s procedures.
Determine whether fuel handling activities are conducted in accordance with 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee’s procedures and programs for radiation protection.
Determine whether the licensee satisfied security plan requirements for fuel movement activities.
Determine whether significant fuel movement or inspection problems are identified and resolved in accordance with the licensee’s procedural controls.
Determine whether the licensee was within TS limits and met procedural requirements before resuming normal operation after fuel movement.

constraints.text: Licensee is required to handle fuel in accordance with the TS and applicable procedures.
Participants must be trained and understand their tasks.
Dry runs may be necessary for operations of potential safety concern or of an unusual nature.
Licensee's fuel handling tools are required to be consistent with TS requirements and licensee procedures.

steps.text: Inspect fuel and control rods in accordance with the TS and their procedures.
Check the control rods to determine their capability to continue to function.
Investigate and resolve any failures in control rods.
Inspect the neutron source for leakage paths if it is removed during refueling.

constraints.text: Fuel movement can cause significant radiological hazards.
Maintain occupational doses as low as reasonably achievable (ALARA).

steps.text: Monitor exits from controlled areas in accordance with the 10 CFR Part 20 and the licensee's procedural requirements.
Use a Geiger Muller (GM) detector for exit surveys.
Check if area radiation levels are so high that a difference of about 0.2 millirem per hour cannot be detected.
Consider shielding the detector area or extending the controlled area to a low radiation area.
Perform surveys for contamination in accordance with licensee procedures.
Be aware of the frequency for the performance of the surveys specified in the licensee’s procedures.
Consider the potential hazard associated with fuel handling activities and the contamination levels of handled objects.
Evaluate the amount of traffic into and out of the controlled area.
Survey licensee personnel, tools, and equipment for contamination upon exit of the work area.

preconditions.text: If direct observation is not possible at the time of the inspection, problems areas may be identified by the review of the licensee’s logs and records, and discussions with responsible personnel for the most recent fuel movement activity.
